title: VLM-Prompted Interactive Video: Bridging Action Fidelity Gaps
teaser: Inject VLM-generated prompts into interactive video models on every frame batch to recover plausible character-environment interactions that the underlying video prior already knows but the interactive variant has lost.
abstract: Interactive video models lack plausible character-environment interactions because the vast majority of their training data comes from Unreal-Engine sources with insufficiently defined character-environment dynamics (characters do not get damaged, do not die, do not perform complex interactions). Working from the hypothesis that the underlying video backbone has internalized a strong prior over realistic interactions, this project re-injects that prior at inference time by prompting the model with VLM-generated guidance whenever a new frame batch is generated. The pipeline trades real-time capability for fidelity, producing offline videos of higher quality and — as a downstream use — pseudo-ground-truth that can be used to fine-tune the interactive variant itself. The student will design the VLM prompting loop, study where in the generation stack the prompts have the most leverage, and quantify the fidelity gain.
milestones: Reproduce a working interactive video model and successfully prompt it to generate the desired videos.
Prompt or fine-tune a VLM that produces high-quality control prompts for the video generation step.
(Optional) Fine-tune the interactive video model using the generated higher-fidelity videos as pseudo-ground-truth.
